DKB Design Knowledge Base
Tools Tracks Writing

Design Knowledge Base  ·  Vol. I

An open notebook for people who design things.

Tools, references, and reading for working designers, the design-curious, and students. Some entries ship as apps; others sit in draft until they're worth your time.

Tools·Tracks·Writing

Index / Tools

8 shipped · 2 planned

  1. 01 Screenshots An open-source collection of product screenshots for inspiration. The original entry; everything else grew from here. Subdomain →
  2. 02 Screenshots MCP An MCP server that pulls relevant screenshots into your project work without leaving the editor. MCP Server →
  3. 03 Design System Comparator Compare design systems side by side to find the best fit for the work in front of you. Subdomain →
  4. 04 Design Documentation A curated archive of design documentation, public and otherwise, gathered for education and reference. Subdomain →
  5. 05 Natural Language ↔ GUI Bidirectional conversion between GUI components and natural-language markdown. A small experiment in describing interfaces in prose. Subdomain →
  6. 06 Web to Figma (Chrome) Capture any web page element and import it directly into Figma as editable layers. Chrome Extension →
  7. 07 Web to Figma (Playwright) Local Chromium-based companion to the extension, for pages that require a session, like logged-in dashboards. Repo →
  8. 08 Chrome Page Designer A Chrome extension for designing and editing web pages directly in the browser. Chrome Extension →
  9. 09 Skills Assessment A self-scoring instrument for designers, against the framework. Notes only at this stage. Planned
  10. 10 Problem Statement Generator A guided exercise for sharpening problem statements before solutioning. Sketch stage. Planned

Reading List / Tracks

5 lanes · in draft

  1. I Framework Process, methods, techniques, principles, toolkits. The how of the work. Planned
  2. II Craft Domain knowledge, tooling, human sciences. Usability, color theory, behavior science. Planned
  3. III Mindset Soft skills, prioritization, time management. The interior side of the practice. Planned
  4. IV Operations Team rituals and the everyday tools that make a design org run. Planned
  5. V Leadership Management, role definitions, strategies for proving design's value. Planned

Outbound / Writing

The repo focuses on practical, instructional content. Case studies, commentary, and longer arguments live on Medium.

Read on Medium →

Maintained by Joe Steinkamp. Open source under the MIT license.

Repo Medium Screenshots

Set in Poppins and Roboto. Built with Jekyll. The Lab Notebook, Vol. I.
